title: Nano-mechanical properties of Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight steels
abstract_summary: The mechanical properties of two duplex low-density steels, Fe-15Mn-10Al-0.8C-5Ni and Fe-15Mn-10Al-0.8C (wt.%), are studied via nano-indentation and DFT to establish hardness contributions of κ-carbides and B2 intermetallics as functions of annealing temperature (500–1050°C) and Ni addition. In the Ni-free sample, elastic properties of κ are compared to B2 Fe3Al-L12, and the role of Mn in κ is explored. Ni-containing samples exhibit higher hardness due to stronger Ni-Al bonds in NiAl-type B2; at ≥900°C, nano-disc B2 hardens ferrite, while at ≤700°C, B2 stringer bands or needle κ form, lowering hardness. Hardness trends are rationalized by interatomic bonding energies.
research_objective: This study aims to determine the hardness contributions of κ-carbides and B2 intermetallics in two duplex Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight steels as functions of annealing temperature and Ni addition, and to rationalize them through atomic bonding energies.
material_systems: B2 intermetallic (NiAl-type in Ni-containing, FeAl-type in Ni-free, also Fe3Al-L12 mentioned)
cuboid κ in γ, needle-like κ in α
main_process_variables: alloy composition: 0 vs 5 wt.% Ni (nominal Fe-15Mn-10Al-0.8C base)
key_sections: Introduction: background on low-density steels, ordered phases (B2, L'1, κ), and motivation for nanomechanical property determination

Materials and Methods: alloy preparation, annealing treatments (500–1050°C), SEM/EDS characterization, nano-indentation protocol, DFT calculation details (PBE-GGA, Quantum ESPRESSO, pseudopotentials, etc.)

Results and Discussion: experimental nano-hardness trends of individual and composite phases (γ, α, B2 stringers, disc-like B2, cuboid and needle κ) vs temperature and Ni content; DFT elastic properties and bonding energy analysis; correlation of hardness to Mn substitution and bond strength
